body.title: Granny: send-keys to CC may be swallowed if session is mid-response
body.intention: I intend that every Granny dispatch to CC.0 results in a sprint-ticket command that CC actually receives and acts on.
body.description:
When Granny sends /sprint-ticket T-xxx via tmux send-keys to claude-main while CC is mid-response (generating output), the keystrokes go into the terminal buffer and are not reliably processed. Observed: T-ibd-intention-field was dispatched at 18:10 and marked in_progress, but the command was never acted on. The ticket stayed orphaned in in_progress until manually reset.

Needs: an idle-check before send-keys (e.g. check if the tmux pane is waiting for input), or a retry mechanism that verifies the ticket transitions from in_progress to done/sprint within a timeout.

This is related to T-granny-orphan-watchdog (already closed) but is the root dispatch-path fix rather than the recovery mechanism.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (_dispatch_cc0), possibly devices/granny/availability.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite N/A; state must be externalized
**Scope boundary:** add idle check or pre-dispatch wait in _dispatch_cc0; do not change ticket routing logic
**Completion criteria:** dispatch to CC.0 while CC is mid-response either waits or queues reliably; dispatched ticket transitions to in_progress and eventually done without manual intervention
